results.possibleIdentity: David Weinflash was an American physicist and technical consultant active from the 1960s through the 1970s. He was a prominent figure at Columbia University, where he served as a Graduate Research Assistant in the Physics Department before completing his Ph.D. in 1971. His doctoral research, titled "Lifetime and Fine Structure of the Metastable Autoionizing (1s2s2p) 4P States of the Helium Negative Ion," was conducted under the supervision of Professor Robert Novick. His technical expertise spanned mathematics, physics, cybernetics, and optics, with a particular focus on the application of lasers, strobes, neon, and ultraviolet light. Weinflash was a technical collaborator with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), the non-profit organization founded by Billy Klüver and Robert Rauschenberg to foster partnerships between the arts and engineering. In this capacity, he applied his knowledge of light and audio-visual conversions to technical-artistic projects. In the early 1970s, he also taught as a visiting lecturer or instructor at Tamkang University in Taiwan; his special topics course there is credited with inspiring Maw-Kuen Wu, a leader in the discovery of high-temperature superconductivity. In his personal life, he has been associated with the musical arts as a cellist in New York City.
